category_title: Students who directly refuse to read through verbal or nonverbal means
minimal_context: The student and interventionist are participating in a one-on-one shared reading session using the book 'Miss Nelson is missing!'.
dialogue:
TUTOR: That last part you read did not make sense. Let's look at the next section of 'Miss Nelson is missing!'.

STUDENT: (Looks down at the desk silently.)

TUTOR: The kids are acting up in the classroom. I will read this part out loud to get us started.

STUDENT: (Remains quiet and avoids looking at the page.)

TUTOR: You wanta start this page?

STUDENT: (Slumps back in the chair with a frown and whispers) No.

options.A: Break the reading goal down into sub-targets over time: first week, remain seated for 10 minutes; second week, remain seated for 90 minutes.
options.B: Model complete sentences as often as possible during the one-on-one shared reading sessions with the student.
options.C: Increase the time allotted for the one-on-one shared reading sessions, extending the lesson duration to 30 minutes rather than 15 minutes.
options.D: Allow the student to select a book from several choices and use a constructivist approach, implementing scaffolds focused on meaning and comprehension.
